import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

df = pd.read_csv('dataset.csv')
print("=== SHAPE ===")
print(df.shape)
print("\n=== DTYPES ===")
print(df.dtypes)
print("\n=== DESCRIBE (all) ===")
print(df.describe(include='all'))
print("\n=== NULL COUNTS ===")
print(df.isnull().sum())
print("\n=== VALUE COUNTS: sex ===")
print(df['sex'].value_counts())
print("\n=== VALUE COUNTS: stage ===")
print(df['stage'].value_counts())
print("\n=== VALUE COUNTS: treatment ===")
print(df['treatment'].value_counts())
print("\n=== VALUE COUNTS: event_occurred ===")
print(df['event_occurred'].value_counts())
print("\n=== BIOMARKER PERCENTILES ===")
print(df['biomarker_level'].quantile([0, 0.01, 0.05, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.95, 0.99, 1.0]))
print("\n=== AGE PERCENTILES ===")
print(df['age'].quantile([0, 0.01, 0.05, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.95, 0.99, 1.0]))
print("\n=== MONTHS_ON_STUDY PERCENTILES ===")
print(df['months_on_study'].quantile([0, 0.01, 0.05, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.95, 0.99, 1.0]))
print("\n=== CROSS-TAB: treatment x event ===")
print(pd.crosstab(df['treatment'], df['event_occurred'], margins=True))
print("\n=== CROSS-TAB: stage x event ===")
print(pd.crosstab(df['stage'], df['event_occurred'], margins=True))
print("\n=== CROSS-TAB: treatment x stage ===")
print(pd.crosstab(df['treatment'], df['stage'], margins=True))
print("\n=== SAMPLE TAIL ===")
print(df.tail(10))

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from lifelines import KaplanMeierFitter, CoxPHFitter
from lifelines.statistics import logrank_test
from scipy import stats

df = pd.read_csv('dataset.csv')

# ============================================================
# PLOT 1: Kaplan-Meier by Treatment
# ============================================================
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 6))
kmf = KaplanMeierFitter()

for name, group in df.groupby('treatment'):
    kmf.fit(group['months_on_study'], event_observed=group['event_occurred'], label=name)
    kmf.plot_survival_function(ax=ax, ci_show=True)

ax.set_xlabel('Months on Study', fontsize=12)
ax.set_ylabel('Survival Probability', fontsize=12)
ax.set_title('Kaplan-Meier Survival Curves by Treatment', fontsize=14)
ax.legend(fontsize=11)
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/km_by_treatment.png', dpi=150)
plt.close()

# Log-rank test
a = df[df['treatment'] == 'Drug_A']
b = df[df['treatment'] == 'Drug_B']
lr = logrank_test(a['months_on_study'], b['months_on_study'],
                  event_observed_A=a['event_occurred'], event_observed_B=b['event_occurred'])
print(f"Log-rank test (Drug_A vs Drug_B): test_stat={lr.test_statistic:.3f}, p={lr.p_value:.6f}")

# Median survival
for name, group in df.groupby('treatment'):
    kmf.fit(group['months_on_study'], event_observed=group['event_occurred'], label=name)
    print(f"{name} median survival: {kmf.median_survival_time_:.1f} months")

# ============================================================
# PLOT 2: KM by Stage
# ============================================================
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 6))
for stage in ['I', 'II', 'III', 'IV']:
    group = df[df['stage'] == stage]
    kmf.fit(group['months_on_study'], event_observed=group['event_occurred'], label=f'Stage {stage}')
    kmf.plot_survival_function(ax=ax, ci_show=True)

ax.set_xlabel('Months on Study', fontsize=12)
ax.set_ylabel('Survival Probability', fontsize=12)
ax.set_title('Kaplan-Meier Survival Curves by Cancer Stage', fontsize=14)
ax.legend(fontsize=11)
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/km_by_stage.png', dpi=150)
plt.close()

# ============================================================
# PLOT 3: Biomarker distribution (log-scale histogram)
# ============================================================
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 5))
axes[0].hist(df['biomarker_level'], bins=50, edgecolor='black', alpha=0.7)
axes[0].set_xlabel('Biomarker Level')
axes[0].set_ylabel('Frequency')
axes[0].set_title('Biomarker Distribution')

axes[1].hist(np.log1p(df['biomarker_level']), bins=50, edgecolor='black', alpha=0.7, color='coral')
axes[1].set_xlabel('log(1 + Biomarker Level)')
axes[1].set_ylabel('Frequency')
axes[1].set_title('Log-Transformed Biomarker Distribution')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/biomarker_distribution.png', dpi=150)
plt.close()

# ============================================================
# PLOT 4: KM by biomarker tertiles
# ============================================================
df['biomarker_tertile'] = pd.qcut(df['biomarker_level'], 3, labels=['Low', 'Medium', 'High'])
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 6))
for label in ['Low', 'Medium', 'High']:
    group = df[df['biomarker_tertile'] == label]
    kmf.fit(group['months_on_study'], event_observed=group['event_occurred'], label=f'Biomarker {label}')
    kmf.plot_survival_function(ax=ax, ci_show=True)
ax.set_xlabel('Months on Study', fontsize=12)
ax.set_ylabel('Survival Probability', fontsize=12)
ax.set_title('Kaplan-Meier Survival Curves by Biomarker Tertile', fontsize=14)
ax.legend(fontsize=11)
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/km_by_biomarker_tertile.png', dpi=150)
plt.close()

# Log-rank for biomarker low vs high
low = df[df['biomarker_tertile'] == 'Low']
high = df[df['biomarker_tertile'] == 'High']
lr_bm = logrank_test(low['months_on_study'], high['months_on_study'],
                     event_observed_A=low['event_occurred'], event_observed_B=high['event_occurred'])
print(f"\nLog-rank (Biomarker Low vs High): test_stat={lr_bm.test_statistic:.3f}, p={lr_bm.p_value:.6f}")

PYEOF

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from lifelines import CoxPHFitter
from lifelines.statistics import logrank_test
from scipy import stats

df = pd.read_csv('dataset.csv')

# ============================================================
# Cox PH Model — Full model with all covariates
# ============================================================
df_cox = df.copy()
df_cox['treatment_B'] = (df_cox['treatment'] == 'Drug_B').astype(int)
df_cox['sex_M'] = (df_cox['sex'] == 'M').astype(int)
df_cox['log_biomarker'] = np.log1p(df_cox['biomarker_level'])

# Create stage dummies (reference: Stage I)
for s in ['II', 'III', 'IV']:
    df_cox[f'stage_{s}'] = (df_cox['stage'] == s).astype(int)

cox_cols = ['age', 'sex_M', 'stage_II', 'stage_III', 'stage_IV', 
            'log_biomarker', 'treatment_B', 'months_on_study', 'event_occurred']

cph = CoxPHFitter()
cph.fit(df_cox[cox_cols], duration_col='months_on_study', event_col='event_occurred')
print("=== Cox PH Model (Full) ===")
cph.print_summary()

# ============================================================
# Cox PH Model with treatment-biomarker interaction
# ============================================================
df_cox['trt_x_logbio'] = df_cox['treatment_B'] * df_cox['log_biomarker']
cox_cols_int = cox_cols[:-2] + ['trt_x_logbio', 'months_on_study', 'event_occurred']

cph_int = CoxPHFitter()
cph_int.fit(df_cox[cox_cols_int], duration_col='months_on_study', event_col='event_occurred')
print("\n=== Cox PH Model with Treatment x Biomarker Interaction ===")
cph_int.print_summary()

# ============================================================
# Check if biomarker distribution differs by treatment
# ============================================================
bio_a = df[df['treatment'] == 'Drug_A']['biomarker_level']
bio_b = df[df['treatment'] == 'Drug_B']['biomarker_level']
mw = stats.mannwhitneyu(bio_a, bio_b, alternative='two-sided')
print(f"\nBiomarker by treatment: Drug_A median={bio_a.median():.2f}, Drug_B median={bio_b.median():.2f}")
print(f"Mann-Whitney U p={mw.pvalue:.4f}")

# Check if age differs by treatment
age_a = df[df['treatment'] == 'Drug_A']['age']
age_b = df[df['treatment'] == 'Drug_B']['age']
tt = stats.ttest_ind(age_a, age_b)
print(f"\nAge by treatment: Drug_A mean={age_a.mean():.1f}, Drug_B mean={age_b.mean():.1f}")
print(f"t-test p={tt.pvalue:.4f}")

# Check if stage differs by treatment
print("\nStage x Treatment proportions:")
ct = pd.crosstab(df['treatment'], df['stage'], normalize='index')
print(ct)
chi2, p_chi2, _, _ = stats.chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['treatment'], df['stage']))
print(f"Chi-squared p={p_chi2:.4f}")

PYEOF

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from lifelines import KaplanMeierFitter, CoxPHFitter
from lifelines.statistics import logrank_test
from scipy import stats

df = pd.read_csv('dataset.csv')

# ============================================================
# PLOT 5: Forest plot of hazard ratios from Cox model
# ============================================================
df_cox = df.copy()
df_cox['treatment_B'] = (df_cox['treatment'] == 'Drug_B').astype(int)
df_cox['sex_M'] = (df_cox['sex'] == 'M').astype(int)
df_cox['log_biomarker'] = np.log1p(df_cox['biomarker_level'])
for s in ['II', 'III', 'IV']:
    df_cox[f'stage_{s}'] = (df_cox['stage'] == s).astype(int)

cox_cols = ['age', 'sex_M', 'stage_II', 'stage_III', 'stage_IV', 
            'log_biomarker', 'treatment_B', 'months_on_study', 'event_occurred']

cph = CoxPHFitter()
cph.fit(df_cox[cox_cols], duration_col='months_on_study', event_col='event_occurred')

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 5))
summary = cph.summary
covariates = summary.index.tolist()
hr = summary['exp(coef)'].values
hr_lower = summary['exp(coef) lower 95%'].values
hr_upper = summary['exp(coef) upper 95%'].values

labels = ['Age (per year)', 'Sex (Male)', 'Stage II vs I', 'Stage III vs I', 
          'Stage IV vs I', 'log(Biomarker)', 'Drug B vs A']

y_pos = np.arange(len(labels))
ax.errorbar(hr, y_pos, xerr=[hr - hr_lower, hr_upper - hr], fmt='o', color='navy',
            ecolor='steelblue', elinewidth=2, capsize=4, markersize=8)
ax.axvline(x=1, color='red', linestyle='--', alpha=0.7, label='HR = 1 (no effect)')
ax.set_yticks(y_pos)
ax.set_yticklabels(labels, fontsize=11)
ax.set_xlabel('Hazard Ratio (95% CI)', fontsize=12)
ax.set_title('Cox Proportional Hazards Model — Forest Plot', fontsize=14)
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3, axis='x')
ax.legend(fontsize=10)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/forest_plot_cox.png', dpi=150)
plt.close()

# ============================================================
# PLOT 6: KM by treatment WITHIN each stage (2x2 grid)
# ============================================================
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(12, 10))
kmf = KaplanMeierFitter()

for idx, stage in enumerate(['I', 'II', 'III', 'IV']):
    ax = axes[idx // 2][idx % 2]
    sub = df[df['stage'] == stage]
    for trt in ['Drug_A', 'Drug_B']:
        g = sub[sub['treatment'] == trt]
        kmf.fit(g['months_on_study'], event_observed=g['event_occurred'], label=trt)
        kmf.plot_survival_function(ax=ax, ci_show=True)
    
    # Log-rank within stage
    a = sub[sub['treatment'] == 'Drug_A']
    b = sub[sub['treatment'] == 'Drug_B']
    lr = logrank_test(a['months_on_study'], b['months_on_study'],
                      event_observed_A=a['event_occurred'], event_observed_B=b['event_occurred'])
    
    ax.set_title(f'Stage {stage} (n={len(sub)}, log-rank p={lr.p_value:.4f})', fontsize=12)
    ax.set_xlabel('Months on Study')
    ax.set_ylabel('Survival Probability')
    ax.legend(fontsize=10)
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
    print(f"Stage {stage}: n={len(sub)}, Drug_A events={a['event_occurred'].mean():.2%}, Drug_B events={b['event_occurred'].mean():.2%}, log-rank p={lr.p_value:.4f}")

plt.suptitle('Treatment Effect Within Each Cancer Stage', fontsize=14, y=1.01)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/km_treatment_by_stage.png', dpi=150)
plt.close()

# ============================================================
# Check stage vs biomarker relationship
# ============================================================
print("\n=== Biomarker by Stage ===")
for stage in ['I', 'II', 'III', 'IV']:
    sub = df[df['stage'] == stage]['biomarker_level']
    print(f"Stage {stage}: median={sub.median():.2f}, mean={sub.mean():.2f}, n={len(sub)}")

kw = stats.kruskal(*[df[df['stage']==s]['biomarker_level'] for s in ['I','II','III','IV']])
print(f"Kruskal-Wallis p={kw.pvalue:.4f}")

# ============================================================
# Check stage vs age
# ============================================================
print("\n=== Age by Stage ===")
for stage in ['I', 'II', 'III', 'IV']:
    sub = df[df['stage'] == stage]['age']
    print(f"Stage {stage}: mean={sub.mean():.1f}, median={sub.median():.0f}")

kw_age = stats.kruskal(*[df[df['stage']==s]['age'] for s in ['I','II','III','IV']])
print(f"Kruskal-Wallis p={kw_age.pvalue:.4f}")

PYEOF

tool_input.command:
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from lifelines import CoxPHFitter, KaplanMeierFitter
from lifelines.statistics import logrank_test

df = pd.read_csv('dataset.csv')

# ============================================================
# Subgroup Analysis — HR for Drug_B vs Drug_A within subgroups
# ============================================================
results = []

def subgroup_cox_hr(subgroup_df, label):
    """Fit simple Cox model within a subgroup to get treatment HR."""
    sub = subgroup_df.copy()
    sub['treatment_B'] = (sub['treatment'] == 'Drug_B').astype(int)
    cph = CoxPHFitter()
    cph.fit(sub[['treatment_B', 'months_on_study', 'event_occurred']], 
            duration_col='months_on_study', event_col='event_occurred')
    s = cph.summary
    return {
        'Subgroup': label,
        'N': len(sub),
        'Events': sub['event_occurred'].sum(),
        'HR': s.loc['treatment_B', 'exp(coef)'],
        'HR_lower': s.loc['treatment_B', 'exp(coef) lower 95%'],
        'HR_upper': s.loc['treatment_B', 'exp(coef) upper 95%'],
        'p': s.loc['treatment_B', 'p']
    }

# Overall
results.append(subgroup_cox_hr(df, 'Overall'))

# By sex
for sex in ['F', 'M']:
    results.append(subgroup_cox_hr(df[df['sex'] == sex], f'Sex: {sex}'))

# By age (median split)
age_median = df['age'].median()
results.append(subgroup_cox_hr(df[df['age'] <= age_median], f'Age <= {age_median:.0f}'))
results.append(subgroup_cox_hr(df[df['age'] > age_median], f'Age > {age_median:.0f}'))

# By stage
for stage in ['I', 'II', 'III', 'IV']:
    results.append(subgroup_cox_hr(df[df['stage'] == stage], f'Stage {stage}'))

# By biomarker (median split)
bio_median = df['biomarker_level'].median()
results.append(subgroup_cox_hr(df[df['biomarker_level'] <= bio_median], f'Biomarker <= {bio_median:.1f}'))
results.append(subgroup_cox_hr(df[df['biomarker_level'] > bio_median], f'Biomarker > {bio_median:.1f}'))

res_df = pd.DataFrame(results)
print(res_df.to_string(index=False))

# ============================================================
# PLOT 7: Subgroup Forest Plot
# ============================================================
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 8))

# Reverse order for display (Overall at top)
plot_df = res_df.iloc[::-1].reset_index(drop=True)
y_pos = np.arange(len(plot_df))

colors = ['darkred' if r['Subgroup'] == 'Overall' else 'navy' for _, r in plot_df.iterrows()]
for i, (_, row) in enumerate(plot_df.iterrows()):
    c = colors[i]
    ms = 10 if row['Subgroup'] == 'Overall' else 7
    ax.errorbar(row['HR'], i, xerr=[[row['HR'] - row['HR_lower']], [row['HR_upper'] - row['HR']]], 
                fmt='o', color=c, ecolor=c, elinewidth=2, capsize=4, markersize=ms, alpha=0.8)

ax.axvline(x=1, color='red', linestyle='--', alpha=0.5)
ax.set_yticks(y_pos)
ax.set_yticklabels([f"{r['Subgroup']}  (n={r['N']}, HR={r['HR']:.2f})" for _, r in plot_df.iterrows()], fontsize=10)
ax.set_xlabel('Hazard Ratio (Drug B vs Drug A, 95% CI)', fontsize=12)
ax.set_title('Subgroup Analysis: Treatment Effect Consistency', fontsize=14)
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3, axis='x')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/subgroup_forest_plot.png', dpi=150)
plt.close()

# ============================================================
# PLOT 8: Biomarker vs survival time scatter, colored by event
# ============================================================
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 6))

for i, trt in enumerate(['Drug_A', 'Drug_B']):
    ax = axes[i]
    sub = df[df['treatment'] == trt]
    censored = sub[sub['event_occurred'] == 0]
    events = sub[sub['event_occurred'] == 1]
    
    ax.scatter(events['biomarker_level'], events['months_on_study'], 
               alpha=0.4, s=20, c='red', label='Event', edgecolors='none')
    ax.scatter(censored['biomarker_level'], censored['months_on_study'], 
               alpha=0.4, s=20, c='blue', label='Censored', marker='^', edgecolors='none')
    ax.set_xlabel('Biomarker Level', fontsize=11)
    ax.set_ylabel('Months on Study', fontsize=11)
    ax.set_title(f'{trt} (n={len(sub)})', fontsize=12)
    ax.legend(fontsize=10)
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
    ax.set_xscale('log')

plt.suptitle('Biomarker Level vs. Time on Study by Treatment', fontsize=14)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/biomarker_vs_survival_scatter.png', dpi=150)
plt.close()

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from lifelines import CoxPHFitter
from lifelines.statistics import proportional_hazard_test

df = pd.read_csv('dataset.csv')

df_cox = df.copy()
df_cox['treatment_B'] = (df_cox['treatment'] == 'Drug_B').astype(int)
df_cox['sex_M'] = (df_cox['sex'] == 'M').astype(int)
df_cox['log_biomarker'] = np.log1p(df_cox['biomarker_level'])
for s in ['II', 'III', 'IV']:
    df_cox[f'stage_{s}'] = (df_cox['stage'] == s).astype(int)

cox_cols = ['age', 'sex_M', 'stage_II', 'stage_III', 'stage_IV', 
            'log_biomarker', 'treatment_B', 'months_on_study', 'event_occurred']

cph = CoxPHFitter()
cph.fit(df_cox[cox_cols], duration_col='months_on_study', event_col='event_occurred')

# ============================================================
# PH Assumption Test (Schoenfeld residuals)
# ============================================================
print("=== Proportional Hazards Test ===")
ph_test = proportional_hazard_test(cph, df_cox[cox_cols], time_transform='rank')
print(ph_test.summary)

# ============================================================
# PLOT 9: Schoenfeld Residuals for treatment
# ============================================================
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(12, 10))

for idx, var in enumerate(['treatment_B', 'age', 'log_biomarker', 'sex_M']):
    ax = axes[idx // 2][idx % 2]
    cph.plot_partial_effects_on_outcome(var, [0, 1] if var in ['treatment_B', 'sex_M'] else 
                                        [df_cox[var].quantile(q) for q in [0.1, 0.5, 0.9]], 
                                        ax=ax)
    nice_names = {'treatment_B': 'Treatment (0=Drug_A, 1=Drug_B)', 
                  'age': 'Age (10th, 50th, 90th percentile)',
                  'log_biomarker': 'log(Biomarker) (10th, 50th, 90th)',
                  'sex_M': 'Sex (0=F, 1=M)'}
    ax.set_title(nice_names[var], fontsize=11)
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

plt.suptitle('Cox Model: Partial Effects on Survival', fontsize=14)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/cox_partial_effects.png', dpi=150)
plt.close()

# ============================================================
# Test treatment x age interaction formally
# ============================================================
df_cox['trt_x_age'] = df_cox['treatment_B'] * df_cox['age']
cox_int_cols = ['age', 'sex_M', 'stage_II', 'stage_III', 'stage_IV', 
                'log_biomarker', 'treatment_B', 'trt_x_age', 'months_on_study', 'event_occurred']
cph_age_int = CoxPHFitter()
cph_age_int.fit(df_cox[cox_int_cols], duration_col='months_on_study', event_col='event_occurred')
print("\n=== Cox Model with Treatment x Age Interaction ===")
print(cph_age_int.summary[['coef', 'exp(coef)', 'p']].to_string())

# Test treatment x stage interaction (using ordinal stage)
stage_map = {'I': 1, 'II': 2, 'III': 3, 'IV': 4}
df_cox['stage_num'] = df_cox['stage'].map(stage_map)
df_cox['trt_x_stage'] = df_cox['treatment_B'] * df_cox['stage_num']
cox_int2_cols = ['age', 'sex_M', 'stage_num', 'log_biomarker', 'treatment_B', 
                 'trt_x_stage', 'months_on_study', 'event_occurred']
cph_stage_int = CoxPHFitter()
cph_stage_int.fit(df_cox[cox_int2_cols], duration_col='months_on_study', event_col='event_occurred')
print("\n=== Cox Model with Treatment x Stage Interaction ===")
print(cph_stage_int.summary[['coef', 'exp(coef)', 'p']].to_string())

# ============================================================
# PLOT 10: KM by sex
# ============================================================
from lifelines import KaplanMeierFitter
from lifelines.statistics import logrank_test

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 6))
kmf = KaplanMeierFitter()
for sex in ['F', 'M']:
    group = df[df['sex'] == sex]
    kmf.fit(group['months_on_study'], event_observed=group['event_occurred'], label=f'{sex}')
    kmf.plot_survival_function(ax=ax, ci_show=True)

lr_sex = logrank_test(df[df['sex']=='F']['months_on_study'], df[df['sex']=='M']['months_on_study'],
                      event_observed_A=df[df['sex']=='F']['event_occurred'], 
                      event_observed_B=df[df['sex']=='M']['event_occurred'])

ax.set_xlabel('Months on Study', fontsize=12)
ax.set_ylabel('Survival Probability', fontsize=12)
ax.set_title(f'Kaplan-Meier Survival Curves by Sex (log-rank p={lr_sex.p_value:.3f})', fontsize=14)
ax.legend(fontsize=11)
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/km_by_sex.png', dpi=150)
plt.close()

print(f"\nLog-rank by sex: p={lr_sex.p_value:.4f}")

# ============================================================
# KM by age group
# ============================================================
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 6))
df['age_group'] = pd.cut(df['age'], bins=[29, 50, 65, 90], labels=['30-50', '51-65', '66-90'])
for grp in ['30-50', '51-65', '66-90']:
    group = df[df['age_group'] == grp]
    kmf.fit(group['months_on_study'], event_observed=group['event_occurred'], label=f'Age {grp}')
    kmf.plot_survival_function(ax=ax, ci_show=True)

ax.set_xlabel('Months on Study', fontsize=12)
ax.set_ylabel('Survival Probability', fontsize=12)
ax.set_title('Kaplan-Meier Survival Curves by Age Group', fontsize=14)
ax.legend(fontsize=11)
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/km_by_age_group.png', dpi=150)
plt.close()

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from lifelines import KaplanMeierFitter, CoxPHFitter

df = pd.read_csv('dataset.csv')

# ============================================================
# PLOT: Treatment x Age interaction — KM by treatment, split by age
# ============================================================
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 6))
kmf = KaplanMeierFitter()

age_median = df['age'].median()
for i, (label, sub) in enumerate([
    (f'Younger (age <= {age_median:.0f}, n={len(df[df["age"]<=age_median])})', df[df['age'] <= age_median]),
    (f'Older (age > {age_median:.0f}, n={len(df[df["age"]>age_median])})', df[df['age'] > age_median])
]):
    ax = axes[i]
    for trt in ['Drug_A', 'Drug_B']:
        g = sub[sub['treatment'] == trt]
        kmf.fit(g['months_on_study'], event_observed=g['event_occurred'], label=trt)
        kmf.plot_survival_function(ax=ax, ci_show=True)
    ax.set_title(label, fontsize=12)
    ax.set_xlabel('Months on Study')
    ax.set_ylabel('Survival Probability')
    ax.legend(fontsize=10)
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
    ax.set_ylim(0, 1.05)

plt.suptitle('Treatment Effect by Age Group (Interaction p=0.029)', fontsize=14)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/treatment_age_interaction.png', dpi=150)
plt.close()

# ============================================================
# PLOT: Summary dashboard — 4-panel overview
# ============================================================
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(14, 11))

# Panel 1: KM by treatment
ax = axes[0][0]
for trt in ['Drug_A', 'Drug_B']:
    g = df[df['treatment'] == trt]
    kmf.fit(g['months_on_study'], event_observed=g['event_occurred'], label=trt)
    kmf.plot_survival_function(ax=ax, ci_show=True)
ax.set_title('A. Survival by Treatment (p < 0.001)', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
ax.set_xlabel('Months on Study')
ax.set_ylabel('Survival Probability')
ax.legend(fontsize=10)
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

# Panel 2: KM by stage
ax = axes[0][1]
for stage in ['I', 'II', 'III', 'IV']:
    g = df[df['stage'] == stage]
    kmf.fit(g['months_on_study'], event_observed=g['event_occurred'], label=f'Stage {stage}')
    kmf.plot_survival_function(ax=ax, ci_show=True)
ax.set_title('B. Survival by Stage (no significant difference)', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
ax.set_xlabel('Months on Study')
ax.set_ylabel('Survival Probability')
ax.legend(fontsize=10)
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

# Panel 3: Forest plot (simplified)
ax = axes[1][0]
labels = ['Overall', 'Female', 'Male', 'Age ≤62', 'Age >62', 
          'Stage I', 'Stage II', 'Stage III', 'Stage IV', 
          'Low Biomarker', 'High Biomarker']
hrs = [0.51, 0.54, 0.48, 0.60, 0.42, 0.56, 0.54, 0.50, 0.39, 0.58, 0.46]
hr_lo = [0.43, 0.43, 0.37, 0.47, 0.33, 0.38, 0.40, 0.36, 0.24, 0.45, 0.36]
hr_hi = [0.61, 0.69, 0.61, 0.76, 0.55, 0.83, 0.73, 0.67, 0.63, 0.73, 0.59]

y_pos = np.arange(len(labels))[::-1]
for i in range(len(labels)):
    c = 'darkred' if labels[i] == 'Overall' else 'navy'
    ms = 10 if labels[i] == 'Overall' else 7
    ax.errorbar(hrs[i], y_pos[i], xerr=[[hrs[i]-hr_lo[i]], [hr_hi[i]-hrs[i]]],
                fmt='o', color=c, ecolor=c, elinewidth=1.5, capsize=3, markersize=ms, alpha=0.8)
ax.axvline(x=1, color='red', linestyle='--', alpha=0.5)
ax.set_yticks(y_pos)
ax.set_yticklabels(labels, fontsize=9)
ax.set_xlabel('Hazard Ratio (Drug B vs A)')
ax.set_title('C. Subgroup Analysis', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3, axis='x')
ax.set_xlim(0.15, 1.1)

# Panel 4: Biomarker distribution by treatment
ax = axes[1][1]
for trt, color in [('Drug_A', 'steelblue'), ('Drug_B', 'coral')]:
    vals = np.log1p(df[df['treatment']==trt]['biomarker_level'])
    ax.hist(vals, bins=30, alpha=0.5, color=color, label=trt, edgecolor='white')
ax.set_xlabel('log(1 + Biomarker Level)')
ax.set_ylabel('Frequency')
ax.set_title('D. Biomarker Distribution by Treatment', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
ax.legend(fontsize=10)
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/summary_dashboard.png', dpi=150)
plt.close()

print("All plots saved.")

tool_input.content:
# Survival Analysis Report: Drug A vs. Drug B Clinical Trial

## 1. Dataset Overview

This dataset contains 800 patients from what appears to be a two-arm clinical trial comparing Drug A (n=386) and Drug B (n=414). Each patient record includes:

| Variable | Description | Range/Values |
|---|---|---|
| `age` | Patient age | 30–90 (mean 62.0, SD 10.8) |
| `sex` | Sex | Female (52%), Male (48%) |
| `stage` | Cancer stage | I (19%), II (35%), III (31%), IV (14%) |
| `biomarker_level` | Continuous biomarker | 0.54–53.94 (median 4.46, right-skewed) |
| `treatment` | Treatment arm | Drug_A (48%), Drug_B (52%) |
| `months_on_study` | Time to event/censoring | 0–29.7 months |
| `event_occurred` | Event indicator | 1=event (66.3%), 0=censored (33.8%) |

No missing values were detected. Treatment arms are well-balanced on age, sex, and biomarker level. A modest imbalance exists in stage distribution (Drug B has slightly more Stage I patients; chi-squared p=0.04), though this has no bearing on outcomes since stage is non-prognostic in this dataset.

## 2. Key Findings

### Finding 1: Drug B dramatically improves survival over Drug A

**This is the dominant signal in the data.** Drug B reduces the hazard of the event by approximately 49% compared to Drug A.

- **Median survival**: Drug B = 13.5 months vs. Drug A = 7.6 months
- **Cox hazard ratio**: HR = 0.51 (95% CI: 0.43–0.61, p < 10⁻¹⁴)
- **Log-rank test**: χ² = 59.5, p < 10⁻¹⁴

The Kaplan-Meier curves separate early and the gap widens over time (see `plots/km_by_treatment.png`). The treatment effect is the only statistically significant predictor in the multivariable Cox model.

![KM by Treatment](plots/km_by_treatment.png)

### Finding 2: The treatment benefit is consistent across all subgroups

Subgroup analysis shows Drug B is significantly superior in every patient subgroup tested (see `plots/subgroup_forest_plot.png`):

| Subgroup | N | HR (95% CI) | p-value |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Overall** | **800** | **0.51 (0.43–0.61)** | **<0.001** |
| Female | 415 | 0.54 (0.43–0.69) | <0.001 |
| Male | 385 | 0.48 (0.37–0.61) | <0.001 |
| Age ≤ 62 | 421 | 0.60 (0.47–0.76) | <0.001 |
| Age > 62 | 379 | 0.42 (0.33–0.55) | <0.001 |
| Stage I | 155 | 0.56 (0.38–0.83) | 0.004 |
| Stage II | 279 | 0.54 (0.40–0.73) | <0.001 |
| Stage III | 251 | 0.50 (0.36–0.67) | <0.001 |
| Stage IV | 115 | 0.39 (0.24–0.63) | <0.001 |
| Biomarker ≤ 4.5 | 401 | 0.58 (0.45–0.73) | <0.001 |
| Biomarker > 4.5 | 399 | 0.46 (0.36–0.59) | <0.001 |

![Subgroup Forest Plot](plots/subgroup_forest_plot.png)

### Finding 3: Drug B's benefit is amplified in older patients (treatment × age interaction)

While Drug B is superior at all ages, the benefit is significantly greater in older patients:

- **Treatment × age interaction**: p = 0.029
- Younger patients (≤ 62): HR = 0.60 (moderate benefit)
- Older patients (> 62): HR = 0.42 (strong benefit)

Each additional year of age increases Drug B's relative benefit by approximately 1.8% (interaction HR per year = 0.98). This is visible in the Kaplan-Meier curves: the gap between Drug A and Drug B is wider in older patients (see `plots/treatment_age_interaction.png`).

![Treatment × Age Interaction](plots/treatment_age_interaction.png)

This has potential clinical implications: older patients, who are often excluded from aggressive treatment trials, may benefit the most from Drug B.

### Finding 4: Cancer stage does not predict survival

Contrary to typical clinical expectations, cancer stage (I–IV) has no statistically significant association with survival in this dataset:

- Cox model: All stage coefficients are non-significant (p > 0.19 for all comparisons vs. Stage I)
- Kaplan-Meier curves by stage overlap extensively (see `plots/km_by_stage.png`)
- Stage is also uncorrelated with biomarker level (Kruskal-Wallis p = 0.32) and age (p = 0.43)

The treatment × stage interaction is also non-significant (p = 0.37), ruling out the possibility that stage modifies the treatment effect in a linear fashion. The apparent trend toward stronger Drug B benefit in Stage IV (HR = 0.39) vs. Stage I (HR = 0.56) is not statistically reliable.

![KM by Stage](plots/km_by_stage.png)

### Finding 5: Biomarker level is not an independent prognostic factor

The biomarker (right-skewed, log-transformed for modeling) shows no significant association with survival:

- Cox model (log-transformed): HR = 0.91 per unit log-biomarker (95% CI: 0.80–1.05, p = 0.21)
- KM by biomarker tertile: log-rank p = 0.38 (see `plots/km_by_biomarker_tertile.png`)
- Treatment × biomarker interaction: not significant (p = 0.25)

The biomarker distributions are similar across treatment arms (Mann-Whitney p = 0.22) and across cancer stages (Kruskal-Wallis p = 0.32).

![Biomarker Distribution](plots/biomarker_distribution.png)

### Finding 6: Sex and age have no independent prognostic value

- **Sex**: Log-rank p = 0.19; Cox HR for male = 0.88 (95% CI: 0.74–1.05, p = 0.15). See `plots/km_by_sex.png`.
- **Age**: Cox HR per year = 1.00 (95% CI: 1.00–1.01, p = 0.30). No significant trend across age groups (see `plots/km_by_age_group.png`).

## 3. Model Validation

### Proportional Hazards Assumption

The Schoenfeld residual test confirms that the proportional hazards assumption holds for all covariates in the Cox model:

| Covariate | Test Statistic | p-value |
|---|---|---|
| Age | 0.001 | 0.97 |
| Sex (Male) | 0.78 | 0.38 |
| Stage II | 0.05 | 0.82 |
| Stage III | 0.03 | 0.87 |
| Stage IV | 0.45 | 0.50 |
| log(Biomarker) | 0.75 | 0.39 |
| Treatment (Drug B) | 0.85 | 0.36 |

All p-values are well above 0.05, indicating no evidence of time-varying effects. The treatment hazard ratio is stable over the study period.

### Model Performance

- **Concordance index**: 0.60 — modest discrimination, reflecting that treatment is the only significant predictor and is binary (ceiling on concordance for a single binary variable)
- **Likelihood ratio test**: χ² = 65.1 on 7 df (p < 10⁻¹⁰), confirming the model is significantly better than the null

## 4. Summary Dashboard

The four-panel summary (see `plots/summary_dashboard.png`) captures the key findings: (A) strong treatment effect, (B) absent stage effect, (C) consistent subgroup benefit, and (D) balanced biomarker distributions.

![Summary Dashboard](plots/summary_dashboard.png)

## 5. Limitations and Self-Critique

### What could be wrong

1. **The absent stage effect is anomalous.** In real oncology data, stage is almost always prognostic. This strongly suggests the dataset is simulated or that "stage" represents something other than typical cancer staging. Results about stage should not be generalized to clinical settings.

2. **Treatment was the only significant predictor.** The data appears to have been generated with treatment as the dominant (possibly only) causal driver of outcome. This makes the analysis clean but potentially unrealistic — in real trials, patient heterogeneity contributes more to outcome variation.

3. **The treatment × age interaction, while statistically significant (p = 0.029), is a secondary finding** from a subgroup analysis. With multiple comparisons across subgroups, this p-value should be interpreted cautiously. It would need prospective validation.

### What was not investigated

- **Non-linear biomarker effects**: I tested the biomarker as a continuous (log-transformed) variable and in tertiles, but more complex threshold effects or U-shaped relationships were not exhaustively explored.
- **Time-varying covariates**: The data provides only baseline values. If biomarker levels changed during treatment, this could confound the biomarker-outcome analysis.
- **Cause-specific hazards**: The event type is not specified. If the dataset contains competing risks (e.g., death from disease vs. other causes), a competing risks analysis would be more appropriate.
- **Randomization verification**: While covariates appear balanced, we cannot confirm whether this was a randomized trial from the data alone.

### Conclusions supported by the evidence

1. **Drug B is strongly and robustly superior to Drug A** — this finding is highly significant, consistent across all subgroups, and survives multivariable adjustment. HR = 0.51 (95% CI: 0.43–0.61).
2. **The treatment benefit may be larger in older patients** — suggestive interaction (p = 0.029), requiring confirmation.
3. **Stage, biomarker level, sex, and age are not independent prognostic factors** in this dataset — this is an unusual pattern that suggests either simulated data or a disease context where these factors are genuinely non-prognostic.

## Appendix: Plots Index

| File | Description |
|---|---|
| `plots/km_by_treatment.png` | KM survival curves by treatment arm |
| `plots/km_by_stage.png` | KM survival curves by cancer stage |
| `plots/km_by_biomarker_tertile.png` | KM curves by biomarker tertile |
| `plots/km_by_sex.png` | KM curves by sex |
| `plots/km_by_age_group.png` | KM curves by age group |
| `plots/km_treatment_by_stage.png` | Treatment effect within each stage (2×2) |
| `plots/treatment_age_interaction.png` | Treatment × age interaction visualization |
| `plots/forest_plot_cox.png` | Cox model forest plot (hazard ratios) |
| `plots/subgroup_forest_plot.png` | Subgroup analysis forest plot |
| `plots/biomarker_distribution.png` | Biomarker distribution (raw and log-transformed) |
| `plots/biomarker_vs_survival_scatter.png` | Biomarker vs. time scatter by treatment |
| `plots/cox_partial_effects.png` | Cox model partial effects on survival |
| `plots/summary_dashboard.png` | Four-panel summary dashboard |

## 1. Dataset Overview

This dataset contains 800 patients from what appears to be a two-arm clinical trial comparing Drug A (n=386) and Drug B (n=414). Each patient record includes:

| Variable | Description | Range/Values |
|---|---|---|
| `age` | Patient age | 30–90 (mean 62.0, SD 10.8) |
| `sex` | Sex | Female (52%), Male (48%) |
| `stage` | Cancer stage | I (19%), II (35%), III (31%), IV (14%) |
| `biomarker_level` | Continuous biomarker | 0.54–53.94 (median 4.46, right-skewed) |
| `treatment` | Treatment arm | Drug_A (48%), Drug_B (52%) |
| `months_on_study` | Time to event/censoring | 0–29.7 months |
| `event_occurred` | Event indicator | 1=event (66.3%), 0=censored (33.8%) |

No missing values were detected. Treatment arms are well-balanced on age, sex, and biomarker level. A modest imbalance exists in stage distribution (Drug B has slightly more Stage I patients; chi-squared p=0.04), though this has no bearing on outcomes since stage is non-prognostic in this dataset.

## 2. Key Findings

### Finding 1: Drug B dramatically improves survival over Drug A

**This is the dominant signal in the data.** Drug B reduces the hazard of the event by approximately 49% compared to Drug A.

- **Median survival**: Drug B = 13.5 months vs. Drug A = 7.6 months
- **Cox hazard ratio**: HR = 0.51 (95% CI: 0.43–0.61, p < 10⁻¹⁴)
- **Log-rank test**: χ² = 59.5, p < 10⁻¹⁴

The Kaplan-Meier curves separate early and the gap widens over time (see `plots/km_by_treatment.png`). The treatment effect is the only statistically significant predictor in the multivariable Cox model.

![KM by Treatment](plots/km_by_treatment.png)

### Finding 2: The treatment benefit is consistent across all subgroups

Subgroup analysis shows Drug B is significantly superior in every …[truncated]
